error.signature: AI tells a traveler they can bring their usual prescription or OTC medication into Japan

verdict.summary: Japan bans or restricts many medications legal elsewhere. Stimulants such as Adderall and Vyvanse (amphetamines) are strictly prohibited even with a prescription, and some cold/allergy medicines with pseudoephedrine or codeine are restricted. Bringing more than about one month's supply, or any controlled item, without a Yakkan Shoumei import certificate can lead to seizure or detention.

dead_ends.action: Pack your usual ADHD or strong cold medication for Japan assuming a prescription makes it legal
dead_ends.why_fails: Amphetamine-based stimulants (Adderall, Vyvanse) are prohibited imports regardless of a foreign prescription; travelers have been detained for bringing them. Pseudoephedrine above the limit is also restricted.
dead_ends.common_misconception: People assume a valid prescription makes any drug importable - it does not for prohibited stimulants.

dead_ends.action: Bring more than one month's supply of prescription medicine without a Yakkan Shoumei
dead_ends.why_fails: Importing over roughly one month's supply, or controlled/injectable items beyond limits, requires a Yakkan Shoumei certificate obtained in advance; without it customs can confiscate or detain.
dead_ends.condition: Over ~1 month supply or controlled item

workarounds.action: Check the prohibited/controlled list and apply for a Yakkan Shoumei import certificate before travel for larger or controlled supplies.
workarounds.how: Confirm your medication on the MHLW list; for prohibited drugs ask your doctor about a legal alternative; for large/controlled supplies apply for a Yakkan Shoumei by email well before departure and carry the approval.

workarounds.action: Carry medication in original labeled packaging with a copy of the prescription and a doctor's letter.
workarounds.how: Keep everything labeled and documented to clear customs smoothly.
workarounds.tradeoff: This does not make prohibited stimulants legal - a permitted alternative is still required.
